findings.description: Phase 1 landed small because the composition machinery already existed (PHASE_PREFIX, nameFor, parseToml, defaultAxesPath) — reuse over reinvention kept every phase to a skill-body or small-CLI change.
Contract-before-execution plus a per-phase antagonist panel held across all 6 phases with zero blocking findings — every unit passed first try, with only a few advisories addressed pre-merge.
findings.evidence: checkins 01-02 across all six branches; 1341 tests green at close

findings.description: After the Phase 1 stacked-merge burn, landing each subsequent phase as its own PR off main made the 6-phase effort clean and revertable with no further stranding.

The plan was not tooling-trackable as authored: letter-suffixed phases (2a-2d), the wrong dependency-line label, and a placeholder manifest meant the router had to renumber and reformat before any phase could dispatch.
findings.evidence: #198 plan-renumber; parse-plan initially saw only phases 1 and 3

findings.description: The Phase 1 stacked PR merged into the wrong base (the parent branch, not main) and stranded the code off main, requiring a rescue branch and a fresh PR.
findings.evidence: #199 merged into plan-renumber; recovered via rebase and #200

findings.description: Test-assertion hygiene against soft-wrapped SKILL.md prose recurred all project: literal-space regexes miss wrapped phrases and presence-anywhere matches drift. All 4 session corrections were assertion-anchoring fixes.
findings.evidence: 4 corrections: soft-wrap regex fix, inline-fix anchor, derive-panel-error start-anchor, header staleness

findings.description: loom-plan or parse-plan should reject or warn on unparseable phase shapes — letter-suffixed ids and the wrong dependency-line label — at authoring time; the drift was silent until the router caught it after the plan had already merged.

Skill-body content tests are now a recurring shape (loom-research-roster, loom-plan-panel, ev-loop-delegation this project) — whitespace-tolerant regex for soft-wrapped phrases and anchoring to distinctive strings should be the default convention.
findings.evidence: 3 skill-body test files added; captured in the agents-nebula test-assertions memory

findings.description: Live-spawn smoke — the whole-effort runtime acceptance gate for research/plan/implementer/fixer agents — is deferred, blocked on an external marketplace-mirror refresh past guild-hirefest (live registry pinned at 73249bd). Source-complete, runtime-pending.
Merged-but-orphaned local branches (.plan, plan-renumber, and the six phase branches) want a gt sync cleanup.
findings.evidence: gt log shows merged branches needing prune
